document.title: Promotion of Nb2O5 on the wustite-based iron catalyst for ammonia synthesis
document.filename: Promotion of Nb2O5 on the wustite-based iron catalyst for ammonia synthesis.pdf

properties.title: Promotion of Nb2O5 on the wustite-based iron catalyst for ammonia synthesis
properties.filename: Promotion of Nb2O5 on the wustite-based iron catalyst for ammonia synthesis.pdf
properties.evidence_text: 题名为“Promotion of Nb2O5 on the wustite-based iron catalyst for ammonia synthesis”，发表于 Applied Surface Science 353 (2015) 17–23。
The WBC shows much higher activity and lower reduction temperature than the traditional magnetite-based catalysts.

properties.display_name: Nb-WBC（Nb₂O₅ 掺杂 Fe₁₋ₓO 基铁催化剂）
properties.evidence_text: For Nb2O5 doped WBC, 0.6 wt% of Nb2O5 was added together with other promoters.
Niobium was selected and investigated as a potential promoter for wustite-based catalyst (WBC) for ammonia synthesis.
Apparently, for both two catalysts, α-Fe forms directly from the reduction of wustite (Fe1−xO) precursor.
Niobium oxide was adopted as a promoter of fused iron catalysts and its effect on reduction behavior and the activities for ammonia synthesis were investigated.
For Nb2O5 doped WBC, 0.6 wt% of Nb2O5 was added together with other promoters.
Table 1 lists the Fe2+/Fe3+ ratio of WBC catalyst as 7.06.
Table 1 lists the Fe2+/Fe3+ ratio of Nb-WBC catalyst as 7.10.

The wustite catalysts were prepared by mixing appropriate proportions of purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters, and melting in an electric resistance furnace at about 1600 ◦C.

The wustite catalysts were prepared by mixing appropriate proportions of purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters, and melting in an electric resistance furnace at about 1600 ◦C. The catalysts were reduced by the mixture of N2 and H2 (H2:N2 = 3:1) at 400, 425, 450, 475 and 500 ◦C for 2, 6, 10, 4 and 2 h, respectively.

properties.display_name: purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters
properties.original_name: purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters
properties.evidence_text: The wustite catalysts were prepared by mixing appropriate proportions of purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters.

properties.display_name: 按比例混合并在约 1600 °C 熔融
properties.original_name: mixing appropriate proportions and melting at about 1600 ◦C
properties.evidence_text: The wustite catalysts were prepared by mixing appropriate proportions of purified magnetite, iron powder, alumina, potassium nitrate, calcium carbonate and other promoters, and melting in an electric resistance furnace at about 1600 ◦C.

properties.original_name: The catalysts were reduced by the mixture of N2 and H2 (H2:N2 = 3:1) at 400, 425, 450, 475 and 500 ◦C for 2, 6, 10, 4 and 2 h
properties.evidence_text: The catalysts were reduced by the mixture of N2 and H2 (H2:N2 = 3:1) at a pressure of 5.0 MPa and with a space velocity of 30,000 h−1 at 400, 425, 450, 475 and 500 ◦C for 2, 6, 10, 4 and 2 h, respectively.

properties.original_name: temperature range of 400–500 ◦C, space velocity of 30,000 h−1 and pressure of 15 MPa
properties.evidence_text: Ammonia synthesis reaction over the samples were investigated in a fixed-bed reactor with an inside diameter of 14 mm in the temperature range of 400–500 ◦C, space velocity of 30,000 h−1 and pressure of 15 MPa.
Ammonia synthesis reaction over the samples were investigated in the temperature range of 400–500 ◦C.
Ammonia synthesis reaction over the samples were investigated with pressure of 15 MPa.
Ammonia synthesis reaction over the samples were investigated with space velocity of 30,000 h−1.
Experiments on reduction performance, activity test and H2-TGA, in situ XRD as well as XPS were carried out to obtain the promotion effect and mechanism involved. H2-TPD was also performed.
The in situ X-ray diffraction patterns of WBC and Nb-WBC catalysts as a function of reduction temperature are shown in Figs. 2 and 3, respectively.
To further investigate the effect of Nb2O5 on the WBC wustite catalyst for ammonia synthesis, XPS experiments were carried out.
H2-TPD is served as a key analysis method for the relative number of active sites in iron catalysts for ammonia synthesis.
Among the reaction temperatures studied, the activity of WBC is marginally higher than that of niobium-doped catalyst.

properties.original_name: the activity of WBC is marginally higher than that of niobium-doped catalyst
properties.evidence_text: Among the reaction temperatures studied, the activity of WBC is marginally higher than that of niobium-doped catalyst.

properties.original_name: activity keeps almost unchanged (or even marginally lower)
properties.evidence_text: Although Nb2O5 improves the reduction of WBC catalyst, the activity keeps almost unchanged (or even marginally lower).
Although Nb2O5 improves the reduction of WBC catalyst, the activity keeps almost unchanged (or even marginally lower).

properties.display_name: 平面结构敏感活性模型
properties.evidence_text: As a structure sensitive reaction, ammonia synthesis activity of iron planes follows the order of [1 1 1] > [2 1 1] > [1 0 0] > [2 1 0] > [1 1 0].

properties.display_name: [1 1 1] 和 [2 1 1] 晶面更有利于氨合成
properties.original_name: [1 1 1] and [2 1 1] planes are favorable for ammonia synthesis
properties.evidence_text: As a structure sensitive reaction, [1 1 1] and [2 1 1] planes are favorable for ammonia synthesis.
N2 dissociation is the rate-determining step for ammonia synthesis.

properties.original_name: Nb2O5 is not favorable for the segregation or formation of solid solutions on the catalyst surface, which are difficult to be reduced
properties.evidence_text: It can be concluded that Nb2O5 partially inhibits the formation or segregation of these solid solution. As we all know, solid solutions are more difficult to be reduced than FeO during the activation or reaction operation of the catalysts. Hence, improved reduction is expected.

properties.display_name: Nb₂O₅ 不促进有利晶面的形成
properties.original_name: niobium does not promote the growth of [2 1 1] plane
properties.evidence_text: However, it seems that niobium does not promote the growth of [2 1 1] plane, which is active for ammonia synthesis.

The catalysts were reduced by the mixture of N2 and H2 (H2:N2 = 3:1) at a pressure of 5.0 MPa and with a space velocity of 30,000 h−1 at 400, 425, 450, 475 and 500 ◦C for 2, 6, 10, 4 and 2 h, respectively.

The in situ X-ray diffraction patterns of WBC and Nb-WBC catalysts as a function of reduction temperature are shown in Figs. 2 and 3, respectively.
To further investigate the effect of Nb2O5 on the WBC wustite catalyst for ammonia synthesis, XPS experiments were carried out.

Among the reaction temperatures studied, the activity of WBC is marginally higher than that of niobium-doped catalyst.

Although Nb2O5 improves the reduction of WBC catalyst, the activity keeps almost unchanged (or even marginally lower).
Although Nb2O5 improves the reduction of WBC catalyst, the activity keeps almost unchanged (or even marginally lower).
As a structure sensitive reaction, ammonia synthesis activity of iron planes follows the order of [1 1 1] > [2 1 1] > [1 0 0] > [2 1 0] > [1 1 0].
As a structure sensitive reaction, ammonia synthesis activity of iron planes follows the order of [1 1 1] > [2 1 1] > [1 0 0] > [2 1 0] > [1 1 0].
As a structure sensitive reaction, [1 1 1] and [2 1 1] planes are favorable for ammonia synthesis.
N2 dissociation is the rate-determining step for ammonia synthesis.

It can be concluded that Nb2O5 partially inhibits the formation or segregation of these solid solution. As we all know, solid solutions are more difficult to be reduced than FeO during the activation or reaction operation of the catalysts. Hence, improved reduction is expected.
However, it seems that niobium does not promote the growth of [2 1 1] plane, which is active for ammonia synthesis.
